operating_state: Total revenue reached $71.2 billion for nine months 2022, increasing 3.3% with operational growth of 7.9% offset by 4.6% negative currency impact. Pharmaceutical segment drove performance with DARZALEX oncology franchise expanding and TREMFYA immunology growth accelerating, while REMICADE declined due to biosimilar competition. MedTech achieved operational growth driven by Interventional Solutions expansion and Vision recovery, while Consumer Health declined despite operational growth.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 90 basis points to 67.3% driven by higher cost of products sold from commodity inflation and currency impacts. Global Supply Chain restructuring program recorded charges while targeting annual pre-tax savings substantially delivered by end of 2022. Operating expenses increased with R&D expanding to 15.1% of sales from 14.5% driven by pharmaceutical portfolio progression.
material_events: Johnson & Johnson completed the $17.1 billion acquisition of ABIOMED on December 22, 2022, paying per share plus contingent value rights based on clinical milestones. The company recorded IPR&D impairment charge for bermekimab development termination and established a $2 billion trust for talc-related liabilities through LTL Management LLC's Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing. Board approved $5.0 billion share repurchase program with $2.0 billion executed while paying dividends.
macro_exposures: Currency fluctuations created 4.6% negative impact on worldwide sales with European operations experiencing significant negative currency impact despite strong operational growth. Russia-Ukraine conflict financial impact remained immaterial with combined operations representing less than 1% of consolidated assets and revenues, though the company suspended advertising and clinical trial enrollment in Russia. Turkey operations now accounted for as highly inflationary beginning Q2 2022.
forward_outlook: Management continues advancing the planned Consumer Health separation as Kenvue within the announced timeframe, with associated incremental tax costs ongoing until legal separation completion. Global Supply Chain restructuring program expected to complete with substantial cost savings delivery. Johnson & Johnson committed to minimum development expenditures across ABIOMED's clinical milestone categories, with potential payments contingent on FDA approvals and achieving aggregate worldwide net sales targets.
uncertainty_profile: Approximately 920 remaining opioid-related cases persist across federal MDL and state courts despite comprehensive settlement agreements, while talc litigation continues with remaining cases post-LTL bankruptcy proceedings. Multiple patent infringement lawsuits across key pharmaceutical products face trial dates scheduled through 2023, with STELARA composition of matter patent expiring September 2023 creating potential significant sales impact. ABIOMED milestone payments face clinical trial execution risk and regulatory approval uncertainty, while ongoing government investigations include DOJ subpoenas for opioid policies and FTC demands regarding contracting practices.
